title: InvenioAI — Advanced RAG for Document Q&A
summary: A RAG system for document Q&A over PDFs combining hybrid dense + sparse retrieval (Qdrant BM42), RAG Fusion multi-query, cross-encoder reranking, and structured Chain-of-Thought reasoning. FastAPI backend, Streamlit frontend, Docker deployment.
modalTitle: InvenioAI — Advanced RAG for Document Q&A
overview: Naive single-vector search over large PDF collections misses paraphrased questions and buries answers in irrelevant chunks. Built as a retrieval system that stays accurate on dense, structurally complex documents and shows its reasoning instead of returning a black-box answer.
architectureText: Built a structure-aware ingestion pipeline that parses PDFs via LlamaParse and preserves heading hierarchy across page breaks, then indexed the chunks into Qdrant with both dense (MMR) and sparse (BM42) vectors so lexical and semantic matches are both covered. Layered RAG Fusion multi-query expansion and a FlashRank cross-encoder reranker on top of retrieval, then drove the final answer through a 4-step Chain-of-Thought protocol (Query Deconstruction, Filtering, Synthesis, Strategy) so responses are grounded and auditable. Added a dual-layer semantic cache (exact match + similarity > 0.90) to cut redundant LLM calls on repeated or paraphrased queries.
codeBlock:
# Core stack
LLM = "Llama 3.3 70B / 3.1 8B (Groq Cloud)"
EMBEDDING = "paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2"  # dense
SPARSE = "Qdrant/bm42-all-minilm-l6-v2-attentions"    # sparse
RERANKER = "FlashRank ms-marco-MiniLM-L-12-v2"        # cross-encoder
results: Retrieval quality: Hybrid dense+sparse search fused with RAG Fusion multi-query catches both semantic and exact-keyword matches that single-vector search misses.
Robust parsing: Structure-aware chunking handles charts, tables, and multi-page sections without losing document outline.
Shipped: Dockerized FastAPI + Streamlit app, live on Hugging Face Spaces and Azure Container Apps.

title: Omnius — Automated Media Intelligence
summary: A media intelligence platform that applies Robert Entman's (1993) framing theory to news articles using an autonomous Pydantic AI research agent and Llama 3.3 / Qwen3 analysis on Groq Cloud, deployed on Azure with a React frontend.
overview: Spotting how different outlets frame the same story - what they blame, what they judge, what they recommend - takes a trained eye and hours of manual reading. Built to automate that analysis using Robert Entman's (1993) academic framing theory as the scoring rubric, instead of an ad-hoc sentiment label.
architectureText: Designed an autonomous Pydantic AI research agent that searches and filters candidate articles via the Tavily API, backed by a hybrid BeautifulSoup4/Tavily scraper for full-text extraction. Wired Groq-hosted LLMs (Llama 3.3 70B, Llama 3.1 8B, Qwen3 32B) to score each article against Entman's four framing pillars - problem definition, causal interpretation, moral evaluation, treatment recommendation - with results validated through Pydantic schemas. Streamed the pipeline live over Server-Sent Events from a FastAPI backend to a React 19 + D3.js frontend, and shipped it as a Docker container on Azure App Service behind an X-API-Key + CORS gate, with GitHub Actions CI/CD pushing to Azure Container Registry.
codeBlock:
# Core stack
AGENT_FRAMEWORK = "Pydantic AI"
LLMs = ["Llama 3.3 70B", "Llama 3.1 8B", "Qwen3 32B"]  # via Groq Cloud
SEARCH = "Tavily API"
SCRAPER = "BeautifulSoup4 + lxml, Tavily Extraction fallback"
results: Autonomous pipeline: The research agent searches, filters, and validates articles end-to-end with no manual curation step.
Live transparency: Server-Sent Events surface scraping and analysis progress in real time instead of a blocking spinner.
Production-deployed: GitHub Actions builds and pushes Docker images to Azure Container Registry on every merge to main, live at omnius-news-analysis.netlify.app.

title: LUCIAN — Lung Carcinoma Histopathology Imaging & Analysis
summary: A lung cancer histopathology classification system built on a fine-tuned ConvNeXt-Base backbone (TensorFlow/Keras), achieving 93.67% test accuracy. Includes Grad-CAM explainability so clinicians can see which tissue regions drove each classification.
modalTitle: LUCIAN — Lung Carcinoma Histopathology Imaging & Analysis
overview: Lung cancer subtyping from histopathology slides is a routine but high-stakes classification task, and a model that can't explain its call is hard for a clinician to trust. Built to classify tissue slides into adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, and benign tissue while surfacing the visual evidence behind each prediction.
architectureText: Fine-tuned a ConvNeXt-Base backbone (pre-trained on ImageNet-1K) with TensorFlow/Keras on a 3,000-image subset of the LC25000 dataset, adding a classification head of global average pooling, a 256-unit dense ReLU layer, 30% dropout, and a 3-class softmax output over 224x224 inputs. Followed the CRISP-DM methodology end-to-end and layered a Grad-CAM pipeline (OpenCV + TensorFlow) on top of the trained model to project gradient-weighted heatmaps back onto each slide, highlighting the regions that drove the classification.
codeBlock:
# Core metrics (80:10:10 split)
TEST_ACCURACY = "93.67%"
F1_MACRO = "93.64%"
PRECISION_MACRO = "93.63%"
RECALL_MACRO = "93.67%"
BACKBONE = "ConvNeXt-Base (TensorFlow/Keras, ImageNet-1K pretrained)"
results: Accuracy: 93.67% test accuracy and 93.64% macro F1 on a held-out 80:10:10 split.
Explainability: Grad-CAM heatmaps give clinicians a visual check on which tissue regions drove each call, not just a bare label.
Shipped: Dockerized Streamlit app on Hugging Face Spaces with automated CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
